commit.message:
feat: 社交闭环三件套 - 关注系统 + 私信系统 + 他人主页

后端：
- 新增 follows 模块：关注/取关/粉丝列表/关注列表/计数，关注写通知
- 新增 messages 模块：发消息/会话聚合/对话/标记已读/未读计数，发消息写通知
- auth.controller.ts：getStats 替换写死的 0，改为从 follows 表读真实计数
- app.module.ts：注册 FollowsModule + MessagesModule

前端：
- 新增 UserProfilePage：他人主页，关注/取关+私信按钮，帖子网格
- 新增 FollowListModal：关注/粉丝弹窗，含关注/取关操作
- 新增 MessagesPage：会话列表 + 单聊气泡，10s 轮询
- 新增 follows.ts / messages.ts API 封装
- PostList/PostDetail/MobilePostDetail：作者头像/名称点击跳转他人主页
- Profile/MobileProfile：following Tab 改为 消息 Tab（私信+通知+关注列表），
  following/followers 数字可点击弹出 FollowListModal
- App.tsx：修正 /users/:userId 路由 + 新增 /messages 路由

commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

Fix installer to check npm prerequisite first

for Claude Code. This ensures we install dependencies in the correct order.

New flow: check npm → install npm if needed → check claude → install claude if needed

Add photo attachments per room, saved consultations improvements, and UI polish

- Photos: tap-to-capture or upload per room, compressed to ~1000px JPEG, stored
  locally as base64; lightbox viewer with prev/next nav; photo count in room
  summary chip; photos stripped from cloud sync to keep Supabase rows small
- Saved consultations: auto-assigns consultation ID when client name is first
  entered (continuous cloud sync starts immediately); open Saved panel now
  pulls from cloud before rendering; added "+ Start New Consultation" footer
  button in the panel
- Active client chip in header shows the current consultation's client name
  with a green dot indicator; updates on load, save, and reset

- updateHeaderChip() called on load, save, reset, and loadConsultation

commit.message:
Fix results screen showing wrong placement order

Players were sorted by seatOrder (seat position), which always put
the host first regardless of when they actually escaped.

Now the server stamps an escapeOrder counter on each player as they
escape (incrementing per player, in resolution order). ResultScreen
sorts by escapeOrder so placement reflects who escaped first.

docs: update README to follow standard-readme spec (#93)

* docs: add standard-readme badge

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update README to follow standard-readme

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* ci: add standard-readme lint check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: use standard-readme pre-commit hook instead of CI job

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update README.md

* Update test.yml

* ci: replace standard-readme hook with wrapper that exits 1 on warnings

The standard-readme CLI always exits 0 even when spec violations are
found (reported as warnings). Replace it with a Node.js wrapper script
that calls the standard-readme binary and exits 1 if any warnings are
present, so pre-commit actually blocks non-compliant READMEs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: ignore appropriate-heading rule in standard-readme lint

The appropriate-heading rule checks that the README title matches the
checkout directory name, which is always wrong in CI environments where
repos are cloned to /code, /workspace, etc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: fix warning detection to exclude summary line

Use a regex requiring whitespace around "warning" to match only formatted
warning lines, not the "⚠ N warning" summary line which also contains
the word "warning".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.2: Fix intent classification, proportional grading, add performance test suite

- Fix 3 intent classification bugs (volatile, buy auto-pair, remove generic "test")
- Switch to proportional grading system (score/max_score) so fewer agents don't penalize grade
- Add 5-phase performance test suite (10 stocks, consistency, speed, edge cases, intent)
- Test results: 10/10 accuracy, 100% consistency, 7/7 intent, 1.7-2.6s speed
- Update README with scorecard example and 6-phase architecture
- Add CHANGELOG.md with full version history

watcher thread overlap — was causing 4-6 identical Telegram alerts
per scan.

Fix settings panel, add character sort options, reduce CPU usage

- Fix gear button not receiving clicks (title bar was eating mouse events
  due to EnableMouse + same frame level; raised gear button above it)
- Fix managedRegions scoping bug (declared after functions that used it)

  manual mouse wheel scroll for WoW 11.x compatibility

  with up/down arrow reordering in custom mode

  recollection every 2 seconds

repository.name: chat
repository.description: A unified TypeScript SDK for building chat bots across Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Discord, and more.

commit.message:
feat: support turn budgets for sub-agents via as_tool_turn_budget

Sub-agents running as tools can now have their own independent turn budget
with self-extension, dynamic instructions, and budget-aware hooks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 4 real bugs, delete 58 false-positive bug tests

Real bugs fixed:
- Cognito _secret_hash: use HMAC-SHA256 instead of plain SHA-256
- StepFunctions _is_ddb_typed: fix premature return inside loop
- CloudFormation Fn::GetAtt: use ".".join(parts[1:]) for dotted attrs
- DynamoDB PutItem: fire MODIFY (not INSERT) when overwriting existing item

Deleted 58 false-positive/feature-gap tests across 8 files:
- test_sts_provider.py: removed 11 validation gap tests (kept 8 passing)
- test_secretsmanager_bugs.py: deleted entirely (8 feature gaps)
- test_lambda_bugs.py: deleted entirely (8 feature gaps)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix real bugs from audit, delete false positive tests

Source fixes (6 real bugs):
- audit/log.py: max_size=0 no longer silently ignored (0 is falsy)
- chaos/fault_rules.py: status_code=0 preserved (was treated as falsy)
- chaos/fault_rules.py: invalid regex raises ValueError not raw re.error
- state/manager.py: path traversal in snapshot names now rejected
- state/manager.py: load(services=[...]) now filters correctly
- state/manager.py: list_snapshots() includes native_services
- extensions/registry.py: _discover_from_env finds ALL plugin subclasses
- extensions/registry.py: on_request returns modified request not None
- extensions/registry.py: mod.plugin type-checked before use
- extensions/compat.py: class named "Extension" no longer skipped
- extensions/compat.py: on_platform_ready skipped if start fails

Deleted false positive tests (25 tests):
- audit log: negative limit (Python slice semantics by design)
- audit log: empty string error, singleton race, timestamp precision,
  duration not measured (all intentional behavior)
- resource browser: multi-region, non-collection attribute (by design)
- chaos: regex partial match (re.search intentional), XML format (feature gap)
- observability: all 5 tests (GIL races, design choices, minor enhancements)
- state manager: JSON serialization data loss (default=str is intentional)

Fix 1: Pattern 3 prepends primary intent before secondary in chain
Fix 2: ATTACH_FIELD_GEOMETRY executor - attach drawn polygon to existing field
Fix 3: Field draw greeting asks new/existing instead of just field name

Fix 6: Dropped facts surfaced to Mouth; mouth prompt v62

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: inline MethodCall/CallExpression in ForOf iterable_expr (+2 correct)

When the for-of iterable is the result of a method call (e.g., mapping.values()),
the codegen was emitting 'mapping.values();' as a standalone discarded statement
and using the iterator temp var in the for-of header. Now we:
1. Mark the MethodCall/CallExpression result as inlined (suppress standalone emission)
2. Add MethodCall/CallExpression to local_exprs so the expression appears directly
   in the for-of header: 'for (const entry of mapping.values())'
